Netflix Corporate, Board of Directors, and Content Teams —

You have officially driven me to the breaking point.

Dead End: Paranormal Park is repulsive, tone-deaf, and emblematic of a platform that has utterly lost respect for paying customers. I will not subsidize content that offends my values, insults my intelligence, and signals the priorities of a company that no longer serves its subscribers. Effective immediately I am canceling my Netflix subscription, and I am calling on every viewer, parent, and former customer who values good taste and principled content choices to do the same.

My demands are simple:

1. Immediately remove Dead End: Paranormal Park from your library.


2. Issue a public apology to subscribers offended by this content and explain how you plan to prevent similar choices in the future.


3. Refund or credit affected subscribers who cancel in protest.


4. Commit to a transparent review of your content slate and decision-making process.



If these demands are not met within 30 days, I will escalate my response beyond cancellation: I will publicly document my reasons across social platforms, contact your major advertisers and licensing partners urging they reconsider working with Netflix, and recruit others to cancel until action is taken. Netflix’s brand will continue to lose customers and partners as long as decisions like this are rewarded with our subscription dollars.

Actions I’ve taken (so far): • I cancelled my subscription today.
• I am posting my cancellation and reasons across social channels and will pin it.
• I will contact your top advertisers and partners to share why I cannot support a platform making these choices.

This is not a plea — it’s a wake-up call. You either course-correct and show respect for your customers, or you will keep losing them. Consider this the first wave.

Sincerely,
A former subscriber who refuses to be ignored
